data.title: Jira bridge sync remediation: parent, tags, link relations
data.description:
## Context

The Jira bridge (`plugins/dso/scripts/bridge/`) tracks only four fields in EDIT events: `priority`, `assignee`, `title`, `description` (see `_handle_edit.py:73-88`). Three DSO-local fields with direct Jira analogs are never synced: `parent_id` (Jira Epic Link), `tags` (Jira Labels), and `blocks`/`depends_on` link relations (Jira Issue Links of type "Blocks"). This leads to silent divergence where a local reparent or retag is invisible in Jira, and vice versa. Additionally, the new `supersedes` relation (scoped in Epic 6f7a-4ba6 expansion) must sync lossily as Jira "Relates" with a local-richer-wins precedence rule on inbound. This epic closes all four bridge sync gaps in one coherent remediation.

## Success Criteria
- `parent_id` synced bidirectionally via Jira Epic Link field. Outbound: local parent-child changes write the Epic Link on next sync. Inbound: Jira Epic Link changes write a local EDIT(parent_id) event. Reducer EDIT handler extended.
- `tags` synced bidirectionally via Jira Labels. Outbound: tag add/remove writes Jira label update. Inbound: Jira label change writes local EDIT(tags) event.
- `blocks` and `depends_on` link relations synced bidirectionally via Jira Issue Links of type "Blocks". Outbound: new LINK event triggers Jira issue-link create. Inbound: new Jira Issue Link triggers local LINK event. UNLINK symmetric.
- `supersedes` relation synced lossily as Jira "Relates" (no native Jira supersedes type). Local `supersedes` value is authoritative: on inbound, if a Jira "Relates" link matches an existing local `supersedes`, local wins; other "Relates" links are imported as local `relates_to`.
- Bridge EDIT event tracking extended in `_handle_edit.py` from 4 fields to 7 (adding parent_id, tags, link-deltas). Comprehensive integration tests covering all four sync directions pass.
- Conflict handling: on simultaneous local + remote change to the same field, bridge raises BRIDGE_ALERT for human resolution (existing pattern).

## Approach
Extend `_handle_edit.py` to track `parent_id`, `tags`, and link-deltas in EDIT tracking. Extend `_outbound_handlers.py` to write Jira Epic Link, Labels, and Issue Links. Extend `_inbound_api.py` to read these Jira fields and emit local EDIT/LINK events. Add precedence rule for `supersedes`-over-`relates` in link handler. Coordinate with Epic 6f7a-4ba6 to ensure `supersedes` relation exists before this epics supersedes-sync work lands.

## Dependencies
- Depends on Epic 1de7-50d4 (resolution/reporter — reducer schema prerequisite for clean EDIT event extension).
- Serialized after Epic 6f7a-4ba6 (extended `ticket link` for `supersedes` relation must exist before this epic can sync it). Overlaps bridge files with 6f7a-4ba6 expansion.

## Out of Scope
- Jira custom field mapping beyond parent/labels/links
- Agent-specific Jira accountId provisioning
- Jira permission escalation handling (operational concern)
